funny_mascot_text: cap off. label off. now crush it like your enemies.
funny_fact: korea recycles 62% of PET bottles. your country could never.

funny_mascot_text: pour it out. rinse it. peel the sticker. you're basically a surgeon now.
funny_fact: koreans rinse so hard, recycling plants barely need to wash anything.

funny_mascot_text: squeeze out every last drop. remove the pump. it's spa day for trash.
funny_fact: the pump mechanism is usually NOT recyclable. surprise!

funny_mascot_text: shake it. wipe it. stuff it in a clear bag. vinyl has feelings too.
funny_fact: dirty vinyl = general waste. clean vinyl = recyclable. the line is thin.

funny_mascot_text: peel off every sticker. every. single. one. yes that one too.
funny_fact: styrofoam takes 500 years to decompose. your grandkids' grandkids will see it.

funny_mascot_text: it touched food. it's dead to recycling now. white bag.
funny_fact: cup noodle containers are almost NEVER recyclable. tragic.

funny_mascot_text: rip off the tape. flatten it. tie it up like a present for the earth.
funny_fact: korea recycles 87% of paper waste. that's an A+ in trash school.

funny_mascot_text: stack them neat. tie them tight. pretend you're a librarian.
funny_fact: thermal receipts are NOT paper recyclable. they're basically plastic in disguise.

funny_mascot_text: rinse it out. flatten it. these get their OWN bin. they're special.
funny_fact: paper cartons are recycled into toilet paper. circle of life.

funny_mascot_text: grease killed it. it's general waste now. pour one out for the box.
funny_fact: the clean lid? recyclable. the greasy bottom? trash. split personality.

funny_mascot_text: rinse it. return it to the store. get ₩100~₩130 back. free money.
funny_fact: soju bottles are reused up to 8 times before recycling. legends.

funny_mascot_text: cap off. rinse. place it gently. it's glass, not a basketball.
funny_fact: broken glass goes in general waste wrapped in newspaper. handle with care.

funny_mascot_text: chug it. rinse it. crush it on your forehead. (don't actually.)
funny_fact: one recycled can saves enough energy to run a TV for 3 hours.

funny_mascot_text: scrape out the tuna. rinse it. your cat would be proud.
funny_fact: steel cans are 100% recyclable and can be recycled forever. immortal.

funny_mascot_text: use it ALL up. poke a hole OUTSIDE. do NOT do this indoors unless you want drama.
funny_fact: unexploded aerosol cans in trash trucks have caused actual fires. yikes.

funny_mascot_text: drain it. bag it. banana peels are food waste royalty.
funny_fact: korea turns food waste into animal feed and compost. your banana lives on.

funny_mascot_text: squeeze out the water. into the yellow bag. easy peasy lemon squeezy.
funny_fact: korea charges food waste by WEIGHT. less water = less money. big brain.

funny_mascot_text: drain the soup. bag the rice. your leftovers have a second life ahead.
funny_fact: 40% of food waste in korea is from households. we can do better.

funny_mascot_text: bones are NOT food waste. animals can't eat them. white bag, please.
funny_fact: the rule is: 'can an animal eat it?' bones = no. your dog disagrees.

funny_mascot_text: eggshells are general waste. i know. i was shocked too.
funny_fact: eggshells, nutshells, and clam shells are all general waste. shell shock.

funny_mascot_text: shells are too hard for composting. white bag. sorry, seafood lover.
funny_fact: after a samgyeopsal party, bones = general waste, lettuce = food waste. split the bill.

funny_mascot_text: coffee grounds are NOT food waste. your morning ritual ends in the white bag.
funny_fact: some people dry coffee grounds and use them as deodorizer. life hack.

funny_mascot_text: tea bags have non-compostable material. white bag. the british would cry.
funny_fact: the string, tag, and bag material make it non-compostable. betrayal.

funny_mascot_text: too fibrous for animal feed. white bag. even trash has standards.
funny_fact: onion skins, corn husks, and chili stems are all general waste. the hard outer life.

funny_mascot_text: used tissues go straight in the white bag. no debate.
funny_fact: wet wipes contain plastic fibers. they're basically tiny plastic sheets.

funny_mascot_text: white bag. no questions asked. we don't judge.
funny_fact: diapers take 500 years to decompose. babies are expensive AND wasteful.

funny_mascot_text: wrap it up so nobody gets cut. then white bag. RIP mug.
funny_fact: ceramics can't be recycled because they contaminate glass recycling. traitor.

funny_mascot_text: receipts look like paper but they're LIARS. white bag.
funny_fact: thermal receipts contain BPA. don't even touch them too long. creepy.

funny_mascot_text: mixed materials = not recyclable. white bag for these confused objects.
funny_fact: a toothbrush is plastic + nylon + rubber. it's having an identity crisis.

funny_mascot_text: find the battery box at your community center or mart. drop and walk away.
funny_fact: one battery can contaminate 600,000 liters of water. scary math.

funny_mascot_text: don't break it. find the collection box. place it like it's a baby.
funny_fact: fluorescent bulbs contain mercury. breaking one indoors = ventilate immediately.

funny_mascot_text: bring it back to any pharmacy. they have a box. full circle.
funny_fact: flushing medicine contaminates water supply. fish don't need your antibiotics.

funny_mascot_text: let it cool. pour it in a bottle. take it to the collection point. chef's kiss.
funny_fact: 1 liter of oil can contaminate 1 million liters of water. ONE. MILLION.

funny_mascot_text: if it's wearable, fold it and drop it in the green bin. fashion recycling.
funny_fact: korea has clothing collection bins everywhere. your old hoodie gets a second life.

funny_mascot_text: this one's too big for bags. register online. pay. stick the number. put it out.
funny_fact: leaving a mattress outside without registration = instant fine. don't risk it.

funny_mascot_text: your sofa had a good run. register it. pay ₩7,000~₩10,000. say goodbye.
funny_fact: a 4-person sofa costs ₩10,000 to dispose. breakup fee.

funny_mascot_text: register it. pay the fee. stick the number on it. chair's last ride.
funny_fact: a single chair costs about ₩2,000~₩3,000 to dispose. cheap funeral.

funny_mascot_text: desks need registration too. pay up. stick the code. leave it outside.
funny_fact: fees depend on size. a big desk can cost ₩8,000+. size matters.

funny_mascot_text: wardrobes over 100cm = ₩15,000. under 100cm = ₩10,000. measure first.
funny_fact: the gangnam-gu portal even lets you pay with KakaoPay. fancy.

category: Bulky Waste (대형폐기물) or Free e-waste pickup
funny_mascot_text: TVs might qualify for FREE e-waste pickup. check first. save money.
funny_fact: e-waste contains gold, silver, and copper. your old TV is a treasure chest.

category: Bulky Waste (대형폐기물) or Free e-waste pickup
funny_mascot_text: big appliances often get FREE pickup. call 1599-0903 first. always.
funny_fact: manufacturers are legally required to take back old appliances in korea. nice.

funny_mascot_text: your bike's last tour. register. pay. stick. goodbye, old friend.
funny_fact: bicycles cost about ₩3,000~₩5,000 to dispose. cheaper than a tune-up.

funny_mascot_text: your suitcase traveled the world. now it travels to the dump. register first.
funny_fact: wheels and metal parts make luggage non-recyclable. mixed material curse.

funny_mascot_text: roll it up. register online. pay. stick the number. put it outside.
funny_fact: carpets can NOT go in clothing donation bins. they're too thick. rejected.

funny_mascot_text: pillows and thick blankets = bulky waste. NOT clothing donation. register.
funny_fact: thin blankets CAN go in clothing bins. thick ones can't. thickness discrimination.
